source.title: Dissociable roles of the central and basolateral amygdala in appetitive emotional learning

confidence_rationale: Provides a direct behavioural dissociation demonstrating that CeA (not BLA) controls appetitive Pavlovian approach conditioning in rats. CeA lesions impaired autoshaping (conditioned approach); BLA lesions did not. This is the key evidence that CeA has a domain-general motivational/mode-prior role that is NOT reducible to harm-driven fear responses — it operates across valences. Combined with Walker & Davis 1997 (CeA for fear-potentiated startle) this creates a cross-valence double dissociation that directly supports SD-035's CeAAnalog as a valence-agnostic salience-to-mode-prior stage. Dorsal and ventral subiculum lesions had no effect, confirming hippocampal independence of this specific conditioning form.
failure_signatures: If CeA lesions specifically impaired only aversive (not appetitive) conditioning, CeAAnalog's mode_prior would need to be restricted to harm-channel inputs rather than the general salience-gated design SD-035 registers.
If BLA lesions also impaired autoshaping (appetitive Pavlovian approach) under any parametric variant, the clean CeA-BLA dissociation underlying SD-035's peer-module design would be weakened.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CeA lesions impair appetitive Pavlovian autoshaping (conditioned approach to food-predictive CS); BLA lesions do not. Dorsal and ventral subiculum lesions have no effect. Contrasts with aversive conditioning (where BLA also required).
mapping.ree_translation: SD-035's CeAAnalog outputs mode_prior as a general salience-responsive mode bias. The Parkinson 2000 result confirms that CeA plays a motivational/mode-prior role even in the ABSENCE of harm (appetitive learning), supporting the design decision that CeAAnalog is NOT a harm-only module. CeA mode_prior should activate for salient motivationally-significant cues generally, with the harm-threat weighting provided by z_harm_a input gating.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Excitotoxic lesion study removes the CeA entirely; SD-035's CeAAnalog is an additive module (not a lesioned-vs-intact comparison). The autoshaping paradigm is a simple Pavlovian approach task without instrumental action selection — REE's SalienceCoordinator integration involves more complex mode selection.
mapping.source_context: Excitotoxic lesion (ibotenic acid and quinolinic acid) in male rats; food-reinforced autoshaping; Eur J Neurosci 2000

confidence_components.notes: Solid Eur J Neurosci paper from the Robbins/Everitt group; paradigm is simple and results are clean. Transfer risk moderate for species; low for architectural interpretation.
